name: Sweden
avoid: October–November — short days, wetter weather, and many summer services in Gotland shut down
Midsummer week — scenic but accommodation and domestic transport prices spike quickly
Kiruna in deep winter without buffer days — snow and wind can disrupt flights and excursions

cities.name: Stockholm
cities.notes: Archipelago capital for Gamla Stan lanes, world-class museums, waterfront walks and easy ferries

cities.name: Gothenburg
cities.notes: Relaxed west-coast city with Haga cafés, archipelago ferries, seafood halls and Liseberg summer energy

cities.name: Malmö
cities.notes: Compact Skåne hub with design-forward food, castle museums, beach promenades and easy Copenhagen access

cities.name: Uppsala
cities.notes: Historic university city for cathedral grandeur, Linnaeus heritage and an easy rail day trip from Stockholm

cities.name: Kiruna
cities.notes: Far-north Lapland base for Icehotel, LKAB mine tours, dog sledding and northern lights nights

cities.name: Visby
cities.notes: Walled Hanseatic town on Gotland with medieval lanes, sea views and Sweden's best summer island mood

itinerary.sim: Comviq, Telia, or Telenor prepaid eSIM/SIMs are the easiest tourist options in Sweden and usually cost about ₹1,000–₹2,000 for enough data for maps, SJ tickets, and aurora forecast apps.

Stockholm.name: Stockholm
Stockholm.note: Three days gives you the strongest first Sweden mix: one old-town day, one museum-heavy Djurgården day, and one palace-or-design day with time for a ferry or viewpoint.

days.theme: Gamla Stan, Royal Quarters & Waterfront Classics
days.note: Keep the day inside Gamla Stan and Södermalm: the palace, cathedral and old-town lanes are minutes apart, then walk or take one metro hop to Monteliusvägen for sunset. Pre-book Royal Palace slots in summer.

activities.name: Gamla Stan self-guided walk — Stortorget, Västerlånggatan and hidden medieval lanes
activities.tip: Start before 9:30am, then loop back after lunch when side alleys are quieter than Stortorget.

activities.name: Stockholm Palace — Royal Apartments and Treasury
activities.tip: Check whether the Royal Apartments are closed for state events before buying tickets.

activities.name: Storkyrkan — Stockholm Cathedral beside the palace
activities.tip: Look for the St George and the Dragon sculpture; entry is quickest between palace timed slots.

activities.name: Monteliusvägen sunset viewpoint — Södermalm skyline walk
activities.tip: Bring a light layer even in summer; the exposed ridge is breezy at golden hour.

days.note: All major stops sit on Djurgården, so do not add cross-city sights today. Take tram 7 or the SL ferry, pre-book Vasa/ABBA/Skansen, and drop one museum if you dislike packed days.

activities.name: Vasa Museum — salvaged 1628 warship in a purpose-built hall
activities.tip: Go first thing and watch the short introductory film before circling the ship levels.

activities.name: ABBA The Museum — interactive Swedish pop-history galleries
activities.tip: Timed tickets are cashless and dynamic; choose an early-afternoon slot after Vasa.

activities.name: Skansen — open-air Sweden museum and Nordic animals
activities.tip: Best in summer or Christmas season; children need a free pre-booked ticket even when admission is free.

activities.name: Djurgården canal and harbour walk / SL ferry back to Slussen
activities.tip: Use a normal SL ticket for the public ferry instead of a separate sightseeing boat.

days.theme: City Hall, Design Stops & Archipelago Feel
days.note: This is a west-to-east waterfront day: start with a timed City Hall tour, cross through the centre to Skeppsholmen, then finish at Fotografiska. Use SL ferries or buses rather than long taxi hops.

activities.name: Stockholm City Hall guided visit — Blue Hall and Golden Hall
activities.tip: Tours sell out around Nobel-week and summer weekends; book the English tour ahead.

activities.name: Skeppsholmen and Moderna Museet exterior campus walk
activities.tip: The island bridges give easy photo angles back to Gamla Stan and Djurgården.

activities.name: Fotografiska — contemporary photography with harbour views
activities.tip: Go late afternoon; the top-floor café is a good vegetarian-friendly dinner backup.

activities.name: Short public ferry ride between Slussen and Djurgården
activities.tip: The ferry is included in an SL period ticket, so this is the cheapest archipelago-flavoured ride.

Uppsala.name: Uppsala
Uppsala.note: Uppsala works best as a focused heritage stop or a long day trip with cathedral, university collections, and one Linnaeus-era garden circuit.

days.note: Keep the morning in the cathedral-university core, then take a local bus or taxi to Gamla Uppsala after lunch. This avoids bouncing between the old royal mounds and the compact centre.

activities.name: Uppsala Cathedral — Scandinavia's tallest church
activities.tip: Visit early for the nave and royal tombs before tour groups arrive from Stockholm.

activities.name: Gustavianum — Uppsala University Museum and Augsburg Art Cabinet
activities.tip: Check reopening hours and timed exhibitions; the anatomical theatre is the signature room.

activities.name: Carolina Rediviva and the Silver Bible exhibition
activities.tip: Pair it with the university quarter walk; leave bags at the lockers if requested.

activities.name: Gamla Uppsala Museum and royal mounds
activities.tip: Take the bus from central Uppsala; the windswept mounds are best before dusk.

Gothenburg.name: Gothenburg
Gothenburg.note: Two days lets you cover the city centre and one west-coast excursion without rushing the seafood halls, Haga streets, or archipelago ferries.

days.note: Stay within Haga, Avenyn and the canal core. Walk Haga first, use a tram to Götaplatsen for the art museum, and book Paddan only if weather is settled.

activities.name: Haga district and Haga Nygata cinnamon-bun stop
activities.tip: Café Husaren's giant kanelbulle is shareable; mornings are calmer than weekend afternoons.

activities.name: Gothenburg Museum of Art — Nordic colourists and Hasselblad Center
activities.tip: The museum pass covers several city museums; closed Mondays.

activities.name: Skansen Kronan hillside viewpoint
activities.tip: The short climb is steep; do it before lunch for the clearest city light.

activities.name: Paddan canal boat tour — low bridges and old harbour
activities.tip: Sit low and keep hands inside; the famous Cheese Slicer bridge is genuinely close.

hotels.name: Sure Hotel by Best Western Center

days.note: Pick a clear-weather archipelago afternoon: Universeum and Feskekörka are central, then tram to Saltholmen for Vrångö/Styrsö ferries. Liseberg is best as an evening add-on, not a full extra park day.

activities.name: Universeum — rainforest, ocean tanks and space labs
activities.tip: Buy timed tickets online; families should start at opening before school groups build up.

activities.name: Feskekörka fish market hall and canal-side lunch
activities.tip: Vegetarians may prefer nearby cafés; use it mainly as an architectural and seafood-market stop.

activities.name: Vrångö or Styrsö southern archipelago ferry with Västtrafik ticket
activities.tip: Choose one island only; Vrångö has easy trails and beaches without needing a car.

activities.name: Liseberg Gardens and evening park atmosphere
activities.tip: Ride passes cost much more; for a short visit, gardens and seasonal lights are enough.

Malmö.name: Malmö
Malmö.note: One full day covers Malmö well because the centre, castle district, and waterfront are compact and easy to combine on foot or by local bus.

days.theme: Old Town Squares, Castle Museums & Seafront
days.note: Malmö is compact: walk the old squares, continue to the castle park and museums, then take a bus or rental bike to Ribersborg for the Turning Torso view. Copenhagen is tempting but belongs on a separate day.

activities.name: Lilla Torg and Stortorget walk — old-town squares and half-timbered façades
activities.tip: Lilla Torg is prettiest before restaurant terraces fill the square at lunch.

activities.name: Malmö Castle and Malmö Museums
activities.tip: One ticket covers the castle, aquarium and exhibitions; it is the city's best rainy-day value.

activities.name: Moderna Museet Malmö
activities.tip: Use it as a short design/art stop if the weather turns; special exhibitions may cost extra.

activities.name: Ribersborg beach promenade and Turning Torso viewpoint
activities.tip: Walk from Västra Hamnen toward the cold-bath house for the cleanest skyline angle.

Visby.name: Visby
Visby.note: Visby is ideal for one summer night focused on the medieval wall, museum quarter, and sea-facing lanes, with a second day only if you want a slower island pace.

days.theme: Medieval Wall, Museum Quarter & Sea Views
days.note: Stay inside and just outside the UNESCO wall: everything is walkable, but cobbles slow you down. In winter, check restaurant and museum hours because many island businesses run reduced schedules.

activities.name: Visby Ringmur and old-town lanes walk
activities.tip: Start at Norderport and follow the wall clockwise for the strongest tower-and-sea views.

activities.name: Gotlands Museum — Picture Stones and Viking-age Gotland
activities.tip: The Picture Stones hall is the priority; it explains the island far better than another ruin stop.

activities.name: St. Mary's Cathedral and ruined church silhouettes
activities.tip: The cathedral is active, so avoid services unless you are attending respectfully.

activities.name: DBW's Botanical Garden and Almedalen waterfront
activities.tip: Best late afternoon when the rose garden and sea wall catch softer light.

Kiruna.name: Kiruna
Kiruna.note: Kiruna needs at least two nights if northern lights matter because one day can go to Jukkasjärvi and the other to a mine tour or weather-flexible aurora activity.

days.note: Base the day around Jukkasjärvi, 20 minutes from Kiruna, so you are not zig-zagging across Lapland. Winter activities sell out and daylight is short; in summer replace aurora with midnight-sun walks.

activities.name: ICEHOTEL day visit in Jukkasjärvi — art suites and ice bar
activities.tip: Book the guided day visit; wear proper boots because interiors stay below freezing even in summer.

activities.name: Jukkasjärvi Church and riverside Sami heritage stop
activities.tip: It is close to ICEHOTEL, so pair them before returning to Kiruna.

activities.name: Guided dog-sled or snowmobile taster near Kiruna
activities.tip: Choose dog sled for quieter wildlife feel; snowmobile tours need warm layers and usually a driving licence.

activities.name: Evening northern lights watch outside the city lights
activities.tip: Check cloud cover as carefully as KP index; a clear low-KP night can beat a cloudy high-KP night.

days.theme: Mine Tour, New Kiruna Centre & Abisko Aurora Night
days.note: This is the ambitious Arctic day: do LKAB and Kiruna's relocated centre by daylight, then take a pre-booked evening Abisko/Aurora Sky Station excursion only when trains/road conditions and the forecast line up.

activities.name: LKAB Visitor Centre mine tour — underground iron-ore megamine
activities.tip: Tours depart by bus from Kiruna tourist centre; book early and bring ID.

activities.name: New Kiruna centre, City Hall Kristallen and Kiruna Church photo stop
activities.tip: The town is being relocated; ask locally where the church move works affect access.

activities.name: Abisko Aurora Sky Station night visit — chairlift above Torneträsk
activities.tip: Runs in aurora season and can close for wind; if cancelled, pivot to a Kiruna-based aurora chase.

connections.method: SJ or Mälartåg direct train from Stockholm C to Uppsala C, about 38–45 minutes

modes.note: SJ/Mälartåg are fastest and most frequent; airport-rail options cost more.
FlixBus can be cheaper but less frequent and slower.
Parking and Stockholm congestion charges can outweigh any saving.

connections.method: SJ high-speed or InterCity train, usually 4.75–5.5 hours via Stockholm or direct services

modes.note: Book early on SJ; direct trains avoid changing in Stockholm.
Budget fallback only; it uses most of a travel day.
Arlanda to Gothenburg can work if timed with an international arrival.
Useful only if continuing into rural west-coast stops.

connections.method: SJ/Öresundståg train down the west coast, about 2.75–3.25 hours

modes.note: Most comfortable city-centre to city-centre option.
FlixBus is cheaper if you do not mind the longer ride.
Straight E6 motorway; parking in Malmö is the main nuisance.

connections.method: Flight via Stockholm or train/bus to Oskarshamn or Nynäshamn plus Destination Gotland ferry

modes.note: Train/bus Malmö→Oskarshamn or Nynäshamn, then ~3 hr ferry to Visby.

modes.duration: 1 hr direct summer; 3–5 hrs via Stockholm
modes.note: Fastest when seasonal direct flights run; otherwise connect through Stockholm.
Drive to Oskarshamn/Nynäshamn and take the car ferry; book vehicle space early in summer.

connections.skipped: No direct rail reaches Gotland because Visby is on an island.

connections.method: Fly Visby→Stockholm→Kiruna, or take the ferry back to the mainland and SJ night train north

modes.note: Usually connects in Stockholm; winter and summer peaks can force an overnight.
Ferry to Nynäshamn, rail into Stockholm, then SJ night train to Kiruna; scenic but slow.

connections.skipped: Driving from Gotland to Lapland is multi-day and poor value unless it is a dedicated road trip.

extras: Sweden is easier to budget than Norway but still pricey: practical city hotels usually land around ₹8,000–₹30,000/night, while Stockholm luxury, Visby summer weekends, and Kiruna winter/ICEHOTEL stays climb much higher.
Use Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmö, and Visby in June–August for long daylight and ferry-friendly weather; save Kiruna for December–March when the aurora season and snow activities are strongest.

SJ is the default for Stockholm–Uppsala and longer intercity train hops, FlixBus is a solid backup on the Gothenburg–Malmö corridor, Destination Gotland ferries handle Visby, and SAS/Norwegian save major time for Gotland and Kiruna jumps.

Vasa Museum, ABBA The Museum, Skansen, Universeum, and the Icehotel are the headline paid stops, so balance them with Sweden's many excellent free walks, cathedral visits, viewpoints, and waterfront promenades.
If northern lights are the main reason for Sweden, keep at least two Kiruna nights because clear skies matter more than forecast apps and one poor-weather evening can wipe out a short stay.
Visby is most rewarding in summer; outside the main season the town is still attractive, but many restaurants, garden spaces, and island services run on shorter schedules.

diet.vegetarian: Very manageable in Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmö and Uppsala: cafés usually mark vegetarian (vegetarisk) dishes, oat milk is standard, and ICA/Coop/Willys/Hemköp stock salads, breads, cheese, plant-based mince and ready meals. Visby is easy in summer but has fewer winter openings; Kiruna has options but self-cater from supermarkets if you have strict needs.
diet.vegan: Best in Stockholm, Gothenburg and Malmö, with HappyCow useful for dedicated vegan spots and bakery/café filters. Look for 'vegansk' labels and ask about dairy/egg in pastries and sauces. In Kiruna/Abisko and Gotland off-season, book restaurants ahead or carry supermarket backup because tour lodges often need advance notice.
phrases: Jag är vegetarian / vegan — I'm vegetarian / vegan
Utan kött och fisk, tack — without meat and fish, please
Har ni veganska rätter? — do you have vegan dishes?
Utan mjölk och ägg — without milk and eggs
Är den här vegansk? — is this vegan?
